summary: Story 1.3 is implementation-ready for Dev. It is clear, sourced, bounded to workspace-level settings and shared editing authorization decisions, and includes focused service plus regression checks.

template_compliance.notes: Required story sections are present, Dev/QA agent sections remain available, and no unfilled template placeholders were found.

executor_assignment.notes: Executor @dev and quality gate @architect are distinct known agents with non-empty quality gate tools. Although the story includes a small migration, the primary work is Laravel application logic and policy/service behavior rather than standalone data architecture.

goal_context_clarity.notes: Story value, Epic 1 relationship, shared editing outcome, and Story 1.1/1.2 sequencing are explicit.

technical_guidance.notes: Migration, model, factory, Tenant relationship, service methods, membership authorization, no-UI selector boundary, and test locations are identified.

reference_effectiveness.notes: References point to specific PRD, architecture, UI architecture, framework, previous story, QA gate, and handoff artifacts.

self_containment.notes: Core requirements, edge cases, scope exclusions, future-mode boundary, and implementation constraints are included directly in the story.

testing_guidance.notes: Focused service test, Story 1.1/1.2 regression tests, route inspection, conditional route/UI regression, and Pint formatting are listed.

security_scope.notes: Tenant-scoped persistence, existing SaaSykit membership, generic cross-tenant denial, and no per-record visibility fields are called out.

coderabbit_integration.notes: Disabled/absent in core-config.yaml; story includes the required skip notice and manual review fallback.

code_intelligence.notes: isCodeIntelAvailable returned unavailable in this session.

anti_hallucination.notes: Technical claims were cross-checked against current local source docs, existing code, migrations, tests, and Laravel 13 Boost documentation for migrations and factories.

dev_readiness.notes: A Dev agent can implement the story as written without needing to invent record CRUD, UI selector behavior, or parallel membership rules.

non_blocking_notes: Laravel Boost database schema returned no tables for the configured connection in this session, so codebase and migration files were used for local structural verification.
Code intelligence is unavailable in this session, so duplicate detection was skipped according to the validate-next-story fallback rule.
No docs/stories/.session-state.yaml exists, so EpicContextAccumulator returned no accumulated session context; validation used Story 1.1, Story 1.2, QA gate, and current handoff artifacts instead.
Story 1.2 still shows Ready for Review in its story file, but the Story 1.3 DevOps handoff records Story 1.2 and the project automation PR as merged; validation proceeds from that operational truth.
Jira child story sync remains pending; local story is the source of truth for implementation until PO/DevOps explicitly syncs it.
